title: lathyrus pannonicus subsp. asphodeloides
intro: Lathyrus pannonicus subsp. asphodeloides, also known as the Pannonian pea or Hungarian pea, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the genus Lathyrus. This plant is a type of legume, known for its medicinal properties, particularly its ability to lower blood pressure and cholesterol levels due to its high content of isoflavones and other bioactive compounds. The health benefits of Lathyrus pannonicus subsp. asphodeloides include its potential to reduce the risk of heart disease, osteoporosis, and certain types of cancer, as well as its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. Herbal preparations of this plant can be consumed as a tea, capsule, or powder, and it is often used in traditional medicine to support cardiovascular health and reduce the symptoms of menopause.
